questions.question: What is the Valley of Flowers known for?
questions.correct_answer: Its wide variety of colourful and exotic flowers
questions.explanation: The Valley of Flowers is famous for hundreds of flower species that bloom during certain months, creating a beautiful natural landscape.

questions.question: In which state of India is the Valley of Flowers located?
questions.explanation: The Valley of Flowers National Park is located in the Chamoli district of Uttarakhand in the Himalayan region.

questions.question: Why is the Valley of Flowers considered a special place?
questions.correct_answer: Because it has rich biodiversity and scenic beauty
questions.explanation: The valley contains hundreds of plant species and rare wildlife, making it a unique natural habitat.

questions.question: What are Living Root Bridges?
questions.correct_answer: Bridges made from the roots of rubber trees
questions.explanation: The roots of rubber trees are trained to grow across rivers and streams to form strong natural bridges.

questions.question: In which Indian state are the Living Root Bridges found?
questions.explanation: These unique bridges are found mainly in the Khasi and Jaintia hills of Meghalaya.

questions.question: Who builds the Living Root Bridges?
questions.explanation: Local villagers guide the roots of trees over many years until they form a stable bridge.

questions.question: Why were Living Root Bridges important for villagers?
questions.correct_answer: They helped people cross rivers safely during heavy rains
questions.explanation: During monsoon seasons, rivers swell and these strong root bridges provide a safe crossing.

questions.question: What is biodiversity?
questions.correct_answer: The variety of plants, animals, and living organisms in an ecosystem
questions.explanation: Biodiversity helps maintain ecological balance and supports life in natural habitats.

questions.question: Why should natural wonders be protected?
questions.correct_answer: To preserve nature and biodiversity for future generations
questions.explanation: Human activities like pollution and deforestation can damage natural ecosystems, so conservation is important.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the chapter 'Spectacular Wonders'?
questions.correct_answer: To highlight the beauty and uniqueness of natural wonders in India
questions.explanation: The chapter describes remarkable natural places and encourages appreciation and protection of nature.

notes.title: Valley of Flowers
notes.content: A beautiful national park in Uttarakhand known for its colourful flowers and rare wildlife species.

notes.title: Living Root Bridges
notes.content: Natural bridges grown from the roots of rubber trees by local communities in Meghalaya.

notes.title: Biodiversity
notes.content: Biodiversity refers to the variety of living organisms in an ecosystem and is important for environmental balance.

notes.title: Environmental Conservation
notes.content: Protecting natural habitats ensures that ecosystems remain healthy and sustainable.

notes.title: Natural Wonders of India
notes.content: India is home to many unique natural wonders that showcase the beauty and power of nature.

facts.fact: The Valley of Flowers is recognised as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
The valley contains around 600 species of flowers.
Living Root Bridges grow stronger over time as the tree roots continue to grow.
Traditional knowledge of local communities helps create sustainable structures like root bridges.
